source.title: Three-Part Testimony Framework
source.summary: An effective testimony structure: (1) IDENTITY - Who you were before Christ (or in a specific struggle). Keep it relatable without glorifying sin. Focus on the emptiness, not the details. (2) ENCOUNTER - How you met or experienced Jesus. Be specific about what changed your understanding. This can be conversion or a specific moment of God's faithfulness. (3) TRANSFORMATION - Who you are now or what God is doing. Emphasize ongoing change, not perfection. Connect it to hope and God's character. Keep the focus on Jesus, not yourself. Make it conversational, not preachy. Length: 2-3 minutes for a full testimony, 30 seconds for a quick version.
